Energy.question: What is energy?
options: The ability to do work or cause change
A type of force only found in batteries
The speed of a moving object
Energy.answer: The ability to do work or cause change

options: The rate at which energy is transferred
The size of a force only
Energy.answer: The rate at which energy is transferred

options: They take millions of years to form

options: They show useful and wasted energy transfers

options: How much input energy is usefully transferred

Energy.answer: How much input energy is usefully transferred

options: A force that only acts in water

options: Friction caused by moving through air
A force that pulls objects underground
Forces.answer: Friction caused by moving through air

options: An upward force from a fluid

Forces.answer: The object stays still or moves at constant speed

The amount of matter in an object
Forces.answer: The force of gravity on an object

options: The amount of matter in an object
The force of gravity on an object
Forces.answer: The amount of matter in an object

Forces.answer: A force that acts when objects touch

Forces.question: What force acts in a stretched rope?

options: They spread weight over a larger area

Science skills.question: What is a hypothesis?
options: A final answer that cannot change

Science skills.question: What is the independent variable?
options: The variable you keep the same

Science skills.question: What are control variables?
options: Variables kept the same to make a test fair
Variables that are always measured in grams
Science skills.answer: Variables kept the same to make a test fair

Science skills.question: Why should results be repeated?
options: To make the experiment longer only

Science skills.question: What is an anomaly?
options: A result that does not fit the pattern
Science skills.answer: A result that does not fit the pattern

Science skills.question: What type of graph is best for continuous data?
What type of graph is useful for comparing categories?
What should a graph axis have?
What does accuracy mean?
options: How close a measurement is to the true value
How many people did the experiment
How quickly the result was taken
Science skills.answer: How close a measurement is to the true value

Science skills.question: What does precision mean?
options: How close repeated measurements are to each other
Science skills.answer: How close repeated measurements are to each other

Science skills.question: What is a conclusion?
options: A summary of what the results show
Science skills.answer: A summary of what the results show

Science skills.question: Which equipment measures volume of a liquid?
What is a fair test?
options: An investigation where only one variable is changed
A test where all variables change
A test that always proves your idea
Science skills.answer: An investigation where only one variable is changed

Science skills.question: What is evidence?
options: Data or observations used to support an idea
Science skills.answer: Data or observations used to support an idea

Science skills.question: Why is a method written clearly?
options: So someone else could repeat the investigation
Science skills.answer: So someone else could repeat the investigation

Cells.question: What are cells?
options: The basic building blocks of living things
Cells.answer: The basic building blocks of living things

Cells.question: What does the nucleus do?
options: Protects the outside of the cell
Cells.answer: Controls the cell

Cells.question: What is the function of the cell membrane?
options: Controls what enters and leaves the cell
Stores genetic material in plants only
Cells.answer: Controls what enters and leaves the cell

Cells.question: What is cytoplasm?
options: A jelly-like substance where many reactions happen
Cells.answer: A jelly-like substance where many reactions happen

Cells.question: Which cell part is found in plant cells but not animal cells?
Cells.answer: Cell wall

Cells.question: What do chloroplasts contain?
Cells.answer: Chlorophyll

Cells.question: What is the role of chloroplasts?
Cells.answer: They absorb light for photosynthesis

Cells.question: What does a vacuole do in a plant cell?
options: Stores cell sap and helps keep the cell firm
Cells.answer: Stores cell sap and helps keep the cell firm

Cells.question: What is a tissue?
options: A group of similar cells working together
Cells.answer: A group of similar cells working together

Cells.question: What is an organ?
options: A group of tissues working together
Cells.answer: A group of tissues working together

Cells.question: Which is an example of an organ?
Cells.answer: Heart

Cells.question: What is an organ system?
options: A group of organs working together
Cells.answer: A group of organs working together

Cells.question: Which organ system moves blood around the body?
Cells.answer: Circulatory system

Cells.question: What is the job of red blood cells?
Cells.answer: Carry oxygen

Cells.question: What are sperm cells adapted for?
Cells.answer: Swimming to an egg cell

Cells.question: What are root hair cells adapted for?
Cells.answer: Absorbing water and minerals

Cells.question: What is diffusion?
options: Movement of particles from high to low concentration
A type of cell division only
Cells.answer: Movement of particles from high to low concentration

Cells.question: What is a microscope used for?
options: Viewing small objects such as cells
Cells.answer: Viewing small objects such as cells

Cells.question: Why are specialised cells useful?
options: They are adapted to do particular jobs
They all do exactly the same job
Cells.answer: They are adapted to do particular jobs

Cells.question: What do mitochondria release energy for?
Cells.answer: Cell activities
